generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary actuation mechanism for MABI MAX 100 robot with defined speed limit of |q̇max| = 75 [°/s]. Operates within closed-loop control inputs processed by active Digital Twin components. Implements safety constraints to prevent over-actuation and ensure alignment between simulated and physical behaviors."
]

instance GearboxMechanicalTransmissionMonitor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder-based monitoring component positioned behind the gearbox to capture mechanical transmission states. Works in conjunction with motor encoder to reduce positional uncertainty in motion tracking."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted on the permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) to provide real-time position tracking data for motion accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Positioned behind the gearbox to monitor mechanical transmission states and reduce positional uncertainty in motion tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance JointFrictionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor integrated into the MABI MAX 100 robot's joint system to capture friction metrics for motion accuracy analysis and diagnostic insights."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointFrictionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance JointFrictionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointFrictionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotionAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model for analyzing motion accuracy using aggregated sensor data traces from position encoders and friction sensors. Implemented in MathWorks Simulink for detailed behavioral analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionAccuracyAnalysisEnabler
]

instance FrictionAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model specifically designed to process joint friction data traces for diagnostic insights. Integrated with SysML IBDs for logical structure representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data traces from physical robot sensors (position encoders and friction sensors) transmitted via MQTT protocol for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted, GearboxPositionDataTransmitted, JointFrictionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ProcessedMotionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Abstraction layer data derived from raw sensor traces after preprocessing (filtering, normalization) for motion accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataTransmissionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight publish-subscribe protocol implementation for real-time transmission of sensor data traces from physical robot to Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing abstraction layers (SysML IBDs) for contextual data trace aggregation from heterogeneous sensor sources."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance MotionAccuracyAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine (MathWorks Simulink) for detailed behavioral analysis of motion accuracy using aggregated sensor data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytical processing module for joint friction data analysis using SysML state machines and MontiArc Automaton for behavioral modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard integration framework for presenting processed data to manufacturing stakeholders (engineers, maintenance personnel)."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for aggregating and preprocessing sensor data traces for further analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight, FrictionDiagnosticInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for monitoring and analyzing processed data to provide insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight, FrictionDiagnosticInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for visualizing processed data through dashboards and interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedMotionDataInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MotionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time analysis of motion accuracy derived from position encoder data traces using physics-based simulation models."
]

instance FrictionDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Diagnostic insights into joint friction patterns identified through analytical processing of sensor data traces."
]

instance VisualizedMotionDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dashboard presentation of processed motion data including joint friction metrics for manufacturing stakeholders."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingRobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the MABI MAX 100 robot system with integrated sensing and actuation components for precision machining tasks."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder, JointFrictionSensor
]

// C5: Components
instance MABIMaxRobotSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Industrial robot (MABI MAX 100) used for precision machining tasks with integrated sensing infrastructure for real-time monitoring."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingRobotEnvironment
]

instance DigitalShadowFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Passive data collection framework aggregating sensor data traces from physical robot without autonomous operation capabilities."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingRobotEnvironment
    base:contains MQTTDataTransmissionEnabler, DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance DigitalTwinProcessingCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Active processing component implementing simulation models (Simulink) and analytical engines for digital representation of physical robot behavior."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingRobotEnvironment
    base:contains MotionAccuracyAnalysisEnabler, FrictionAnalysisEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates real-time, data-driven representations with temporal synchronization between physical and digital components, as implied by the abstract's description of digital shadows as 'real-time, data-driven representations.' Sensor data is streamed via **MQTT** (as referenced in Section IV.A), which introduces a low-latency, publish-subscribe communication protocol suitable for time-sensitive applications. While explicit synchronization frequencies or granularity requirements are not detailed, the use of **MQTT** suggests a scalable, event-driven temporal model capable of handling high-frequency data streams. The integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) further implies support for dynamic time-scale modeling, where behavior can be simulated at varying resolutions depending on system requirements. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the real-time nature of the data flow, though precise thresholds (e.g., sub-millisecond, millisecond, or second-level granularity) are not specified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is orchestrated within a **heterogeneous, multi-tool ecosystem** that integrates **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) with **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) and **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java). This constellation follows an **architecture pattern** rooted in **component-connector models**, where systems are decomposed into modular, interoperable units with well-defined interfaces. Resource management is implicitly handled through the **tool-specific workflows** of these integrated environments, though explicit **orchestration mechanisms** (e.g., event-driven, query-based, or middleware-based) are not detailed. The document highlights that a **comprehensive system description** may require **cross-tool coordination**, suggesting that system-wide coordination relies on **semantic alignment** between modeling notations and tools rather than a predefined orchestration framework. No explicit mention is made of **API layers**, **service meshes**, or **distributed coordination protocols** (e.g., Kubernetes, gRPC), leaving these aspects as implicit requirements of the integrated toolchain."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The development follows methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between phases involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which model components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavioral modeling is augmented via **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed behavior is simulated using tools like **MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages (C++, Java), or other simulation frameworks**. Lifecycle governance integrates heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and ensures alignment across **multiple modeling notations and tools**, addressing interoperability challenges through standardized interfaces and data exchange protocols. The lifecycle transitions from abstract conceptual models to executable digital representations, with iterative refinement guided by **MBSE principles** and **systems engineering best practices**.

"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system emphasizes **traceability** of data generation, ensuring that contextual data traces are systematically linked to their physical sources. **Validation and verification** are implicitly addressed through the comparison of **simulated data versus measured data**, enabling identification of deviations and model inaccuracies. While explicit **uncertainty quantification** or **error correction mechanisms** are not detailed, the sensor arrangement (e.g., dual encoders) is noted to **reduce positional uncertainty**, implying a structured approach to mitigating measurement errors. Quality assurance processes are inferred to rely on **cross-model consistency checks** and **data aggregation methodologies**, though specific protocols (e.g., statistical validation, uncertainty propagation) are not explicitly outlined in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this system relies on **MQTT** as the primary communication protocol, connecting physical sensors (e.g., in manufacturing) to the digital shadow. The architecture follows a **component-connector paradigm** (as referenced in ADLs like SysML IBDs [17], [27]), where components represent physical devices or data sources, and connectors define data flows. The system interfaces with **Siemens SINUMERIK controllers** for industrial automation. No explicit details on **network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols** (e.g., TLS) are provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this system leverages **MathWorks Simulink** for executable models and simulation, alongside **dashboard-based visualization tools** for monitoring and analysis. The system is designed to integrate heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives, suggesting a **modular deployment model** (e.g., cloud/edge hybrid). However, no explicit details on **hosting platforms (cloud/on-premise/edge), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or availability/reliability assurances** are provided in the documents. The use of SysML and AAS implies a structured but unspecified deployment environment."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **heterogeneous integration** through **MBSE tools** and **multi-stakeholder perspectives**, requiring seamless data exchange across **different modeling notations** and **tool ecosystems**. While no specific **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) or **data exchange protocols** (beyond the implied use of **MQTT** for sensor data streaming in Section IV.A) are explicitly mentioned, the document underscores the need to integrate **passive digital shadows** (one-way data flows) with **active Digital Twin components** to enable bidirectional data processing. Horizontal integration is further constrained by the **passive nature of digital shadows**, which act as data aggregation points without autonomous operation, necessitating **active software systems** to interpret and process incoming data. The absence of explicit references to **IoT platforms**, **enterprise systems**, or **legacy databases** suggests that integration is primarily confined to the **MBSE and simulation toolchain**, with no vertical alignment to broader enterprise architectures described."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The document references **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** as the primary standard for **architecture description** (functional/logical layers) and **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for digital representation frameworks (implied in manufacturing contexts). No other standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, IEEE P2418) or protocols (XML, JSON) are explicitly mentioned. Interoperability is addressed via **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML + Simulink), but no formal compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 23247) or certification requirements are detailed.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **safety-critical constraints** for robotic operations, explicitly defined in the context of a **MABI robotic arm (Max-100)**. The system enforces a **safety cell** that restricts the maximum working range to **[-90°°, 90°°]**, ensuring operator and equipment protection within predefined spatial boundaries. Additionally, the system limits the **maximum achievable speed** to **|q˙ max| = 75°/s**, mitigating dynamic risks such as collisions or instability during motion.

For **data integration and communication**, the Digital Twin leverages **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** via the **Mosquitto server** ([52]), which supports **TLS encryption** for secure data transmission, though explicit mention of TLS configuration or authentication protocols (e.g., OAuth, JWT) is absent from the provided documents. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [50]) is referenced as a **semantic model** for asset representation, but its **security mechanisms** (e.g., access control, role-based policies) are not detailed in the context of the Digital Twin system.

The **RAMI 4.0 reference architecture** ([49], [50]) outlines a **multi-layered cyber-physical system (CPS) framework**, where security considerations are implied through **vertical (layered) and horizontal (cross-layer) integration**, though specific **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., intrusion detection, encryption policies) are not elaborated in the provided documents.

**Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied through **hardware-level constraints** (e.g., speed limits, safety cells) but are not explicitly described as part of a broader **safety-critical system design** (e.g., ISO 13849, IEC 61508 compliance). The **ThingsBoard IoT platform** ([53]) is mentioned as an open-source solution for **IoT data aggregation**, but its **security features** (e.g., role-based access control, audit logging) are not detailed in the context of the Digital Twin system.

In summary, the provided documents emphasize **physical safety constraints** (speed, range) and **semantic modeling** (AAS, RAMI 4.0) but lack explicit details on **data security protocols**, **privacy-preserving techniques**, or **formal risk assessment methodologies** for the Digital Twin system."
]
